description.en: A rustic Galician sourdough famous for huge, irregular holes and a thick, crackling crust, topped with a twisted knot (moña) pinched by hand. A rye starter and a near-100% hydration dough give it real tang and several days of shelf life for toasting.
description.de: Ein rustikales galicisches Sauerteigbrot, berühmt für riesige, unregelmäßige Poren und eine dicke, krachende Kruste, gekrönt von einem handgezwickten Knoten (moña). Ein Roggensauerteig und fast 100% Hydration geben ihm echten Biss und tagelange Haltbarkeit zum Toasten.
description.es: Un pan de masa madre rústico gallego, famoso por sus enormes alvéolos irregulares y una corteza gruesa y crujiente, coronado con una moña retorcida a mano. Una masa madre de centeno y una hidratación cercana al 100% le dan un toque ácido real y varios días de vida.
description.fr: Un pain au levain rustique de Galice, réputé pour ses énormes alvéoles et sa croûte épaisse et craquante, coiffé d'un nœud torsadé (moña) pincé à la main. Un levain de seigle et une hydratation proche de 100% lui donnent un vrai peps acidulé et plusieurs jours de tenue.

origin.note: Recently granted Protected Geographical Indication status; must contain a minimum share of native Galician flours.

ingredients.note: masa madre de centeno, a rye sourdough starter

yield.shape: round loaf topped with the traditional twisted 'moña' knot

keeping.note: The rye sourdough and thick crust give it real keeping power; it's traditionally baked in large loaves meant to last several days.

summary.en: Combine both flours with the water, holding back the starter, and rest at least 2 hours.
Work in the active rye sourdough starter, then give the dough stretch-and-folds every 30 minutes for 3-4 hours.
Shape into a ball, pinching and lifting a piece of dough on top into the traditional twisted knot.
Place seam-up in a floured banneton and refrigerate at least 8-10 hours.
Bake at 250°C with steam for 15 minutes, then drop to 200°C for another 45 minutes.
Rest on a rack at least 20 minutes before cutting so the huge holes don't collapse.

source.adaptation: The rye starter is assumed at roughly 100% hydration to split into total-flour baker's percentages.
